text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Margaret Osei-Bonsu
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
SEX: Female
SURGEON: [Attending HPB Surgical Oncologist, redacted]
SUBMITTING SERVICE: Hepatopancreaticobiliary Surgery
CLINICAL HISTORY: 66-year-old female with biopsy-confirmed borderline resectable pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the pancreatic head/uncinate process (cT3 N0 M0, Stage IIA at diagnosis). Treated with neoadjuvant modified FOLFIRINOX (8 cycles) followed by concurrent Xeloda-based chemoradiation (50.4 Gy in 28 fractions). Preoperative CA 19-9: 52 U/mL (normalized from baseline 1,200 U/mL). Restaging imaging showed tumor reduction to 2.3 cm with SMA abutment decreased to 80-90 degrees. Now presenting for definitive surgical resection.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN IDENTIFICATION
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: Pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure) — en bloc resection including pancreatic head and uncinate process, distal common bile duct, duodenum (C-loop and proximal jejunum to ligament of Treitz), gallbladder, and regional lymph node packet.
Specimen B: Intraoperative frozen section, superior mesenteric artery (SMA) margin — submitted separately at time of surgery.
Specimen C: Intraoperative frozen section, pancreatic neck margin — submitted separately at time of surgery.

NOTE: Specimen B and Specimen C were evaluated intraoperatively (see Frozen Section Diagnosis below).

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE REDACTED — TO BE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
DATE OF RECEIPT IN PATHOLOGY: [DATE REDACTED]
DATE OF FINAL REPORT: [DATE REDACTED]
PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Surgical Pathologist, redacted]
CO-SIGNED BY: [Gastrointestinal Pathology Fellow, redacted]

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
FROZEN SECTION DIAGNOSES (INTRAOPERATIVE)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen B — SMA Margin, Frozen Section:
NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY. Fibro-adipose and neural tissue with no evidence of invasive carcinoma. (Confirmed on permanent sections.)

Specimen C — Pancreatic Neck Margin, Frozen Section:
NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY. Pancreatic parenchyma with mild chronic pancreatitis-like changes and no evidence of invasive carcinoma. No dysplastic ductal epithelium identified. (Confirmed on permanent sections.)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A — Pylorus-Preserving Pancreaticoduodenectomy:

Received fresh and then fixed in formalin, the specimen consists of a pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy. The pancreatic head and uncinate process measure 7.2 x 5.8 x 4.3 cm in aggregate. The attached segment of duodenum measures 22 cm in length with a luminal diameter ranging from 2.0 to 3.5 cm. The proximal jejunum is present measuring 8 cm. The distal common bile duct measures 3.0 cm in length and 0.5 cm in diameter; the bile duct lumen is patent. The gallbladder is received intact, measures 8.0 x 3.2 cm, and contains dark green bile and no calculi. The gallbladder wall thickness is 0.2 cm; no mucosal lesions are identified.

The specimen is inked and serially sectioned according to the standard Leeds protocol for pancreaticoduodenectomy specimens. The anterior surface is inked blue, posterior/retroperitoneal (uncinate) surface is inked black, SMA margin is inked green, bile duct margin is inked red.

On serial sectioning of the pancreatic head perpendicular to the long axis, there is an ill-defined, tan-white, firm, stellate mass in the pancreatic head/uncinate process region measuring 2.1 x 1.8 x 1.5 cm (greatest dimension 2.1 cm). The mass has a gritty texture on cut surface and shows central fibrosis with scattered pale yellow foci suspicious for residual tumor or acellular mucin pools, interspersed with denser white fibrotic areas, consistent with treated carcinoma with treatment response. The mass does not grossly extend to the mucosal surface of the duodenum. The tumor appears to abut the periductal soft tissue adjacent to the main pancreatic duct; the main pancreatic duct measures 0.3 cm in diameter adjacent to the mass and is not grossly obstructed.

Distance from mass to surgical margins (gross estimate):
- Posterior/retroperitoneal margin: 2 mm (closest grossly)
- SMA/uncinate margin: 3 mm
- Anterior surface: 5 mm
- Pancreatic neck transection margin: grossly clear (>10 mm)
- Bile duct transection margin: grossly clear (>10 mm)
- Proximal and distal duodenal/jejunal margins: grossly uninvolved

The peripancreatic and periduodenal soft tissue contains multiple tan-white lymph nodes identified and submitted entirely. Regional lymph node stations are designated according to the Japan Pancreas Society classification and the operative specimen inventory.

Representative sections submitted as follows:
- A1–A4: Pancreatic head mass in relation to posterior margin and SMA margin (entirely submitted in four cassettes, perpendicular sections)
- A5–A6: Pancreatic head mass in relation to anterior surface and superior mesenteric vein groove
- A7: Pancreatic neck transection margin (shave section, entirely submitted)
- A8: Bile duct transection margin (shave section, entirely submitted)
- A9: Proximal duodenal margin
- A10: Distal jejunal margin
- A11–A12: Uninvolved pancreatic parenchyma (tail side, perpendicular sections)
- A13: Gallbladder (fundus, body, cystic duct margin)
- A14–A32: All identified peripancreatic and periduodenal lymph nodes, individually submitted and labeled by anatomic station (18 lymph nodes identified in total)

Total cassettes: 32

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Sections of the pancreatic head mass show a moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma with histologic features of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma demonstrating treatment response. The residual viable tumor consists of irregular glandular structures and small nests of malignant epithelial cells embedded within an extensively desmoplastic stroma. The tumor glands display moderate-to-severe nuclear pleomorphism, enlarged nuclei with irregular nuclear membranes, prominent nucleoli, increased nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios, and amphophilic to pale cytoplasm with focal intraluminal mucin production. Mitotic figures are present.

Treatment effect is prominent and widespread. Extensive areas of hypocellular fibrosis, acellular mucin pools lined by degenerative flat epithelium, stromal hyalinization, hemosiderin deposition, and scattered foamy macrophages are identified, all consistent with post-treatment regression. Viable tumor cells are estimated to comprise approximately 40% of the original tumor bed area, with the remaining 60% represented by treatment-related fibrosis, acellular mucin pools, and necrotic debris. This corresponds to a College of American Pathologists (CAP) histologic tumor regression grade of 2 (moderate response; residual cancer with evident tumor regression).

Perineural invasion: Present. Extensive perineural invasion is identified, with multiple small nerve bundles and larger neural structures containing infiltrating carcinomatous glands and single tumor cells. Perineural invasion is identified in the peripancreatic soft tissue adjacent to the uncinate process and in proximity to the SMA margin soft tissue.

Lymphovascular invasion: Present. Lymphovascular invasion is identified in the form of neoplastic emboli within thin-walled vascular spaces in the peripancreatic adipose tissue.

Duodenal wall involvement: Tumor extends into the periduodenal soft tissue but does not breach the duodenal muscularis propria or involve the duodenal mucosa.

Common bile duct: No carcinoma identified in the submitted sections of the common bile duct wall adjacent to the primary tumor. Mild reactive changes in the bile duct epithelium.

Main pancreatic duct: Mild ductal ectasia adjacent to the tumor. No high-grade pancreatic intraepithelial neoplasia (PanIN-3) identified at the margin.

Uninvolved pancreatic parenchyma: Sections show moderate pancreatic lobular atrophy with interlobular fibrosis and mild chronic pancreatitis-like changes, consistent with post-treatment effect and background obstructive pancreatitis. Islets of Langerhans are scattered and reduced in number.

Gallbladder: Sections show mild chronic cholecystitis with Rokitansky-Aschoff sinuses. No carcinoma, dysplasia, or adenoma identified.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
MARGIN STATUS
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

All surgical margins are negative for invasive carcinoma (R0 resection):

1. Posterior/retroperitoneal margin: NEGATIVE. Closest approach of viable tumor: 2 mm (perpendicular measurement). Acellular mucin pools and treatment-effect fibrosis extend to within 1 mm of this margin; however, no viable malignant cells are identified at or within 1 mm of the inked margin. The posterior margin tissue contains extensive perineural invasion in proximity, but the margin itself is free of viable tumor.

2. SMA/uncinate process margin: NEGATIVE. Closest viable tumor: 3 mm (perpendicular measurement). Perineural invasion is noted at 1.5 mm from the inked margin, but no viable tumor cells are present at the margin.

3. Anterior surface: NEGATIVE. Closest viable tumor: 5 mm.

4. Pancreatic neck transection margin: NEGATIVE. No invasive carcinoma, no high-grade PanIN. Margin is greater than 10 mm from viable tumor.

5. Bile duct transection margin: NEGATIVE. No invasive carcinoma identified. Greater than 10 mm from tumor.

6. Proximal duodenal/gastric margin: NEGATIVE. No carcinoma.

7. Distal jejunal margin: NEGATIVE. No carcinoma.

Overall margin status: R0 (negative margins, curative-intent resection)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
LYMPH NODE EVALUATION
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Total lymph nodes examined: 18
Lymph nodes positive for metastatic carcinoma: 2 of 18 (pN1)

Positive nodes:
- Station 13 (posterior pancreaticoduodenal): 1 of 2 nodes positive; largest metastatic deposit 4 mm; no extranodal extension identified.
- Station 14 (superior mesenteric artery): 1 of 3 nodes positive; metastatic deposit 2 mm; no extranodal extension.

Negative nodes by station:
- Station 5 (suprapyloric): 0/2
- Station 6 (infrapyloric): 0/2
- Station 8a (common hepatic artery): 0/2
- Station 12b (hepatoduodenal ligament): 0/2
- Station 17 (anterior pancreaticoduodenal): 0/3
- Peripancreatic (undesignated): 0/2

Extranodal extension: NOT IDENTIFIED

Note: Metastatic deposits show no significant treatment effect or regression, in contrast to the primary tumor, suggesting viable nodal disease at the time of resection.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Primary Diagnosis:
PANCREATIC DUCTAL ADENOCARCINOMA, MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED (GRADE 2)

Site: Pancreatic head / uncinate process
Tumor size: 2.1 cm greatest dimension (reduced from 3.4 cm at initial diagnosis)
Histologic grade: G2 (moderately differentiated)
Histologic treatment response (CAP grading): Grade 2 — Moderate response (approximately 40% viable tumor remaining; 60% fibrosis/acellular mucin pools)
Perineural invasion: PRESENT (extensive)
Lymphovascular invasion: PRESENT
Duodenal wall involvement: Present (periduodenal soft tissue only; muscularis propria and mucosa not involved)
Margins: All NEGATIVE (R0 resection)
- Posterior/retroperitoneal margin: Negative (2 mm)
- SMA/uncinate margin: Negative (3 mm)
- Anterior surface: Negative (5 mm)
- Pancreatic neck: Negative (>10 mm)
- Bile duct: Negative (>10 mm)
- Duodenal/jejunal margins: Negative
Regional lymph nodes: 2 of 18 POSITIVE (pN1); no extranodal extension
Distant metastasis: Not assessed on this specimen

Pathologic Staging (AJCC Cancer Staging Manual, 8th Edition):
pT3 pN1 pMX — Stage IIB

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Immunohistochemistry (performed on Specimen A, formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded tissue, sections from the primary tumor block):

- CK7: POSITIVE (diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining in tumor glands)
- CK19: POSITIVE (diffuse, strong)
- CK20: NEGATIVE
- CDX2: NEGATIVE
- MUC1: POSITIVE (luminal and cytoplasmic)
- MUC5AC: FOCALLY POSITIVE (consistent with prior EUS-FNB)
- TTF-1: NEGATIVE
- Synaptophysin: NEGATIVE
- Chromogranin A: NEGATIVE
- SMAD4/DPC4: RETAINED (positive nuclear staining in tumor cells; consistent with prior biopsy and NGS findings showing SMAD4 wild-type)
- p53: ABERRANT OVEREXPRESSION (strong, diffuse nuclear positivity in >80% of tumor nuclei; consistent with TP53 p.R248W gain-of-function mutation identified on prior NGS)
- Ki-67 (MIB-1): Proliferative index approximately 25-30% in viable tumor areas

MMR IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (pending at time of initial sign-out; results to be issued as addendum):
- MLH1: Pending
- MSH2: Pending
- MSH6: Pending
- PMS2: Pending

Note: Microsatellite status was previously assessed as microsatellite stable (MSS) by NGS on the preoperative EUS-FNB specimen; MMR IHC on the resection specimen is being performed for confirmatory purposes and institutional protocol compliance. Results will be issued as an addendum upon completion.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ADDITIONAL COMMENTS
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. The pathologic findings are consistent with moderately differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with significant but incomplete treatment response (CAP grade 2) following neoadjuvant modified FOLFIRINOX (8 cycles) and concurrent cape-based chemoradiation. The reduction in tumor size from 3.4 cm at diagnosis to 2.1 cm at resection (38% reduction by greatest dimension) is consistent with a partial pathologic response.

2. The extensive perineural invasion identified in the resection specimen, including near the SMA margin, is characteristic of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma and is a recognized adverse histopathologic feature associated with locoregional recurrence risk.

3. Lymphovascular invasion is present, consistent with the identified nodal metastases.

4. SMAD4/DPC4 retained expression on IHC is consistent with the molecular profile identified on preoperative NGS (SMAD4 wild-type), which may confer a slightly more favorable prognosis compared to SMAD4-deleted tumors.

5. Acellular mucin pools at margins: Although acellular mucin pools approach within 1 mm of the posterior margin, these do not contain viable tumor cells. Per current College of American Pathologists and ISGPS consensus guidelines, acellular mucin pools without viable carcinoma cells are not considered positive margins and do not alter the R0 designation.

6. Correlation with preoperative imaging, clinical history, and prior biopsy results (EUS-FNB, pancreatic head, moderately differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, KRAS G12V, TP53 R248W, CDKN2A homozygous deletion, SMAD4 wild-type by prior NGS) is recommended for multidisciplinary tumor board discussion.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL PATHOLOGIC STAGING SUMMARY (AJCC 8th Edition)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

pT: pT3 — Tumor greater than 4 cm in greatest dimension OR tumor involving the celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and/or common hepatic artery (Note: applied as pT3 per clinical staging convention for tumors >2 cm but <=4 cm with peripancreatic soft tissue extension; final staging per multidisciplinary correlation)
pN: pN1 — Metastasis in 1 to 3 regional lymph nodes
pM: pMX — Not assessed (no distant sites in this resection specimen)
Histologic grade: G2 (moderately differentiated)
Resection status: R0 (all margins negative)
Pathologic stage group: Stage IIB (pT3 N1 M0)

This report has been reviewed and electronically signed by the attending surgical pathologist.

[Electronically signed — Attending Pathologist, Department of Surgical Pathology]
[Report finalized — see electronic signature log for timestamp]

ADDENDUM (PENDING): MMR immunohistochemistry results will be reported as a separate addendum upon completion of staining and interpretation.
